clusters.title: UN calls for reparations to remedy the 'historical wrongs' of trafficking enslaved Africans
clusters.neutral_headline: UN Calls For Reparations For Historical Wrongs

items.title: UN calls for reparations to remedy the 'historical wrongs' of trafficking enslaved Africans
items.standfirst: The U.N. General Assembly adopted a resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans "the gravest crime against humanity" and calling for reparations.
items.content: The U.N. General Assembly adopted a resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans "the gravest crime against humanity" and calling for reparations.(Image credit: Pamela Smith)

items.title: UN names African slave trade 'gravest crime against humanity'
items.standfirst: In tonight's edition, the UN General Assembly designated the transatlantic African slave trade as "the gravest crime against humanity," in a move advocates hailed as a step towards healing and possible reparations. Also, in a visit to top African trading partner Algeria, Italy's prime minister, Giorgia Meloni, said the two nations would work to increase deliveries of Algerian gas. And the conflict in the Middle East risks crippling Kenya's flower industry.
items.content: In tonight's edition, the UN General Assembly designated the transatlantic African slave trade as "the gravest crime against humanity," in a move advocates hailed as a step towards healing and possible reparations. Also, in a visit to top African trading partner Algeria, Italy's prime minister, Giorgia Meloni, said the two nations would work to increase deliveries of Algerian gas. And the conflict in the Middle East risks crippling Kenya's flower industry.

items.title: UN votes to recognise enslavement of Africans as 'gravest crime against humanity'
items.standfirst: The landmark resolution calls for an apology and contributions to a reparations fund, without specifying an amount.
items.content: The landmark resolution calls for an apology and contributions to a reparations fund, without specifying an amount.

items.title: UN votes to describe slave trade as ‘gravest crime against humanity’
items.standfirst: Members call for reparatory justice as landmark resolution aims for ‘political recognition at the highest level’The United Nations has voted to describe the transatlantic chattel slave trade as the “gravest crime against humanity” and called for reparations as “a concrete step towards remedying historical wrongs”.The landmark resolution passed on Wednesday was backed by the African Union (AU) and the Caribbean Community (Caricom). It had been proposed by Ghana’s president, John Dramani Mahama, who said: “Let it be recorded that when history beckoned, we did what was right for the memory of millions who suffered the indignity of slavery.” Continue reading...
items.content: Members call for reparatory justice as landmark resolution aims for ‘political recognition at the highest level’The United Nations has voted to describe the transatlantic chattel slave trade as the “gravest crime against humanity” and called for reparations as “a concrete step towards remedying historical wrongs”.The landmark resolution passed on Wednesday was backed by the African Union (AU) and the Caribbean Community (Caricom). It had been proposed by Ghana’s president, John Dramani Mahama, who said: “Let it be recorded that when history beckoned, we did what was right for the memory of millions who suffered the indignity of slavery.” Continue reading...

items.title: UN designates slave trade as ‘gravest crime against humanity’ despite US opposition
items.standfirst: The UN General Assembly on Wednesday designated the transatlantic African slave trade as "the gravest crime against humanity" in a move advocates hailed as a step towards healing and possible reparations. The United States, Israel and Argentina opposed the measure while Britain and EU member states abstained.
items.content: The UN General Assembly on Wednesday designated the transatlantic African slave trade as "the gravest crime against humanity" in a move advocates hailed as a step towards healing and possible reparations. The United States, Israel and Argentina opposed the measure while Britain and EU member states abstained.

ai_summary: UN General Assembly adopts resolution on slave trade
Resolution declares slave trade a grave crime against humanity
Reparations fund proposed for affected communities
African Union and Caribbean Community support resolution
Resolution aims for political recognition of historical wrongs

items.standfirst: Ukrainian president says peace deal proposed by US includes handing over land to Russia. What we know on day 1,492 Continue reading...
items.content: Ukrainian president says peace deal proposed by US includes handing over land to Russia. What we know on day 1,492 Continue reading...

items.title: US ties Ukraine security guarantees to Donbas withdrawal, Zelensky says
items.standfirst: Ukrainian President Volodomyr Zelensky said the US is conditioning post-war security guarantees on Kyiv ceding the Russian-occupied Donbas, as Washington pushes for a swift end to the war. Zelensky warned such terms would weaken Ukraine’s defences and embolden Moscow, amid uncertainty over future US support despite continued military aid.
items.content: Ukrainian President Volodomyr Zelensky said the US is conditioning post-war security guarantees on Kyiv ceding the Russian-occupied Donbas, as Washington pushes for a swift end to the war. Zelensky warned such terms would weaken Ukraine’s defences and embolden Moscow, amid uncertainty over future US support despite continued military aid.

clusters.title: Trump official refers New York AG Letitia James for prosecution again
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump Official Refers New York AG For Prosecution

items.title: Trump official refers New York AG Letitia James for prosecution again
items.standfirst: A Trump administration official has made new criminal referrals against New York Attorney General Letitia James to federal prosecutors in Miami and Chicago for two cases of possible homeowner's insurance fraud, sources told CBS News.
items.content: A Trump administration official has made new criminal referrals against New York Attorney General Letitia James to federal prosecutors in Miami and Chicago for two cases of possible homeowner's insurance fraud, sources told CBS News.

items.title: Trump housing chief requests new criminal investigation into Letitia James
items.standfirst: Bill Pulte reportedly urges examination of alleged fraud as New York attorney general’s lawyer attacks ‘vendetta’ The Trump administration’s federal housing director Bill Pulte is asking prosecutors to investigate New York attorney general Letitia James for insurance fraud, according to criminal referrals reported by MS Now and CBS News.The referrals to prosecutors in Florida and Illinois allege that James may have committed mortgage insurance fraud. The allegations center on applications made to Universal Property Insurance company, which is based in Florida, and Allstate in Illinois. Continue reading...
items.content: Bill Pulte reportedly urges examination of alleged fraud as New York attorney general’s lawyer attacks ‘vendetta’ The Trump administration’s federal housing director Bill Pulte is asking prosecutors to investigate New York attorney general Letitia James for insurance fraud, according to criminal referrals reported by MS Now and CBS News.The referrals to prosecutors in Florida and Illinois allege that James may have committed mortgage insurance fraud. The allegations center on applications made to Universal Property Insurance company, which is based in Florida, and Allstate in Illinois. Continue reading...

ai_summary: Trump administration official refers Letitia James for prosecution
Allegations of insurance fraud against New York AG
Referrals made to federal prosecutors in Miami and Chicago
Letitia James' lawyer calls allegations a vendetta
Investigation into alleged insurance fraud ongoing
